source.title: Reviewed educational septic flank pain case
source.citation: Local reviewed educational case file for Diagnostician demo content.
source.raw_text: De-identified educational case source assembled and reviewed for demo validation.

case.title: Fever and flank pain with hypotension
case.chief_complaint: Fever, rigors, confusion, and right flank pain.
teaching_points: Fever, flank pain, hypotension, and altered mentation should trigger early sepsis treatment.
Hydronephrosis with infected urine suggests a source-control problem, not only uncomplicated infection.
Definitive management requires antibiotics, resuscitation, and urgent decompression.
curation_notes: Manually authored and reviewed demo case with treatment, observation, imaging, microbiology, and consult paths.

facts.label: Opening concern
facts.value: The patient is brought in for fever, shaking chills, confusion, and severe right flank pain.

provenance.quote: Fever, rigors, confusion, and right flank pain.

facts.label: Symptom progression
facts.value: Dysuria began three days ago, followed by flank pain yesterday and confusion this morning.

provenance.quote: Dysuria for three days, flank pain yesterday, confusion this morning.

facts.label: Associated symptoms
facts.value: She has nausea and poor oral intake but no cough, chest pain, or abdominal guarding.

provenance.quote: Nausea and poor intake; no cough, chest pain, or abdominal guarding.

facts.label: Relevant history
facts.value: History includes type 2 diabetes, recurrent kidney stones, and chronic kidney disease stage 3.

provenance.quote: Type 2 diabetes, recurrent nephrolithiasis, CKD stage 3.

facts.label: Home medications
facts.value: She takes metformin, lisinopril, and acetaminophen as needed.

provenance.quote: Metformin, lisinopril, acetaminophen as needed.

facts.label: Triage vitals
facts.value: Temperature is 39.2 C, blood pressure 84/50 mmHg, heart rate 126/min, respiratory rate 24/min, oxygen saturation 96% on room air.

facts.label: Focused exam
facts.value: She is confused but arousable, has dry mucous membranes, and has marked right costovertebral angle tenderness.

provenance.quote: Confused but arousable, dry mucous membranes, right CVA tenderness.

facts.label: CBC
facts.value: White blood cell count is 22.4 x10^9/L with neutrophil predominance.

provenance.quote: WBC 22.4 x10^9/L with neutrophil predominance.

facts.label: Chemistry and lactate
facts.value: Creatinine is 2.1 mg/dL from a baseline of 1.2, bicarbonate 18 mmol/L, and lactate 4.3 mmol/L.

provenance.quote: Cr 2.1 from 1.2, bicarbonate 18, lactate 4.3.

facts.label: Urinalysis
facts.value: Urinalysis shows positive nitrites, large leukocyte esterase, many white cells, and bacteria.

provenance.quote: Positive nitrites, large leukocyte esterase, many WBCs and bacteria.

facts.label: Cultures
facts.value: Blood and urine cultures later grow Escherichia coli susceptible to ceftriaxone.

provenance.quote: Blood and urine cultures grew E. coli susceptible to ceftriaxone.

facts.label: Renal ultrasound
facts.value: Renal ultrasound shows moderate right hydronephrosis and a 9 mm echogenic focus near the ureteropelvic junction.

provenance.quote: Moderate right hydronephrosis with 9 mm echogenic focus near the UPJ.

facts.label: Noncontrast CT
facts.value: CT confirms a 9 mm proximal right ureteral stone with upstream hydronephrosis and perinephric stranding.

provenance.quote: 9 mm proximal right ureteral stone with hydronephrosis and perinephric stranding.

facts.label: Initial resuscitation
facts.value: She receives 30 mL/kg crystalloid and broad-spectrum IV antibiotics within the first hour.

provenance.quote: 30 mL/kg crystalloid and broad-spectrum IV antibiotics given within one hour.

facts.label: Repeat vitals
facts.value: After fluids, blood pressure improves to 96/58 mmHg, heart rate remains 112/min, and mental status is slightly clearer.

provenance.quote: After fluids BP 96/58, HR 112, mental status slightly clearer.

facts.label: Urology consult
facts.value: Urology recommends urgent decompression because infection is present above an obstructing ureteral stone.

provenance.quote: Urology recommended urgent decompression for infected obstructing ureteral stone.

facts.label: Source control
facts.value: A right ureteral stent is placed with drainage of cloudy urine from the renal pelvis.

provenance.quote: Right ureteral stent placed; cloudy urine drained from renal pelvis.

facts.label: Dangerous differential
facts.value: A urinary source with shock physiology should remain on the dangerous differential, especially when flank pain and stone history are present.

provenance.quote: Fever, flank pain, hypotension, and stone history support dangerous urinary source consideration.

facts.label: Hint 1
facts.value: Do not stop at infection; ask whether there is a reason infected urine cannot drain.

provenance.quote: Infection plus impaired drainage changes management.

facts.label: Hint 2
facts.value: The highest-yield next step after antibiotics and fluids is imaging that evaluates the collecting system.

provenance.quote: Renal imaging is key when infection and obstruction are both possible.

facts.label: Final diagnosis
facts.value: Obstructive pyelonephritis with sepsis from a proximal right ureteral stone.

provenance.quote: Final diagnosis: obstructive pyelonephritis with sepsis due to proximal right ureteral stone.
